deep_dive.title: Argus: Metric Panoramic 3D Reconstruction for Indoor Scenes
deep_dive.abstract: Metric feed-forward 3D reconstruction for panoramic data remains under-explored due to the lack of large-scale panoramic RGB-D training data. We present Realsee3D, a hybrid dataset of 10K indoor scenes (1K real, 9K synthetic) with 299K panoramic viewpoints and precise metric annotations, and Argus, a feed-forward network trained on it for metric panoramic 3D reconstruction. In the sparse unordered capture setting of Realsee3D, a poorly chosen coordinate anchor can cause global pose drift. Argus addresses this with a learned covisibility module that selects the geometrically optimal reference view to anchor the metric world frame. To further improve multi-task learning, we decompose the bidirectional pixel-to-world mapping into interpretable sub-steps with per-step supervision and cross-coordinate joint constraints, reinforcing geometric consistency across prediction branches. On the Realsee3D benchmark, Argus achieves state-of-the-art metric performance in camera pose estimation, depth estimation, and point cloud reconstruction. Project page: https://argus-paper.realsee.ai.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: metric panoramic 3D reconstruction for indoor scenes
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 室内场景的度量全景3D重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 室内场景的度量全景3D重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 室内场景的度量全景3D重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Argus achieves metric feed-forward 3D reconstruction from sparse panoramic views using a learned covisibility module and a large hybrid dataset.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Argus针对全景稀疏视图的度量3D重建，提出了大规模混合数据集Realsee3D和端到端网络Argus。通过可学习的共视模块选择最优参考视图锚定度量世界坐标系，并将双向像素到世界映射分解为可解释子步骤，实现了从稀疏全景输入直接预测度量深度和相机位姿。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Argus针对全景稀疏视图的度量3D重建，提出了大规模混合数据集Realsee3D和端到端网络Argus。通过可学习的共视模块选择最优参考视图锚定度量世界坐标系，并将双向像素到世界映射分解为可解释子步骤，实现了从稀疏全景输入直接预测度量深度和相机位姿。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Three-dimensional scene reconstruction from images or point clouds

deep_dive.title: Bridging 3D Gaussians and Semantic Occupancy for Comprehensive Open-Vocabulary Scene Understanding from Unposed Images
deep_dive.abstract: Comprehensive 3D scene understanding from sparse, unposed images requires a model to recover renderable geometry, open-vocabulary semantics, and free/occupied 3D space without relying on external camera calibration. Recent feed-forward Gaussian methods improve pose-free reconstruction and semantic rendering, but their Gaussian primitives are mainly optimized through image-space objectives and remain weakly constrained in unobserved regions. We propose \textit{COVScene}, a pose-free semantic Gaussian framework that couples renderable Gaussian primitives with a dense semantic occupancy field through differentiable volumetric lifting. Instead of converting Gaussians to voxels only at evaluation time, COVScene lifts the predicted semantic Gaussians inside the training computation graph, so volumetric regularization provides gradients to Gaussian opacity, geometry, and semantic features. The framework combines a semantic-aware Geometry Transformer, multi-task Gaussian decoding, geometric foundation distillation, and occupancy entropy regularization to support novel view synthesis, open-vocabulary semantic querying, and semantic occupancy prediction within a single representation. Experiments on ScanNet and ScanNet++ show that COVScene maintains competitive rendering quality, improves open-vocabulary segmentation, and achieves stronger semantic occupancy prediction than the self-supervised baseline without direct voxel-level supervision.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Bridging 3D Gaussians and semantic occupancy for scene understanding from unposed images
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将3D高斯与语义占据结合，用于无位姿图像的场景理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将3D高斯与语义占据结合，用于无位姿图像的场景理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将3D高斯与语义占据结合，用于无位姿图像的场景理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: COVScene couples renderable 3D Gaussian primitives with a dense semantic occupancy field via differentiable volumetric lifting for pose-free 3D scene understanding and novel view synthesis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: COVScene提出无位姿语义高斯框架，通过可微体积升维将可渲染高斯原语与稠密语义占据场耦合。在无需外部标定的情况下，从稀疏无位姿图像中恢复可渲染几何、开放词汇语义和自由/占据空间。该方法利用高斯原语进行高效渲染，同时利用占据场约束未观测区域，实现了综合性的无位姿3D场景理解。实验表明在新视角合成和语义分割上均取得优异性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: COVScene提出无位姿语义高斯框架，通过可微体积升维将可渲染高斯原语与稠密语义占据场耦合。在无需外部标定的情况下，从稀疏无位姿图像中恢复可渲染几何、开放词汇语义和自由/占据空间。该方法利用高斯原语进行高效渲染，同时利用占据场约束未观测区域，实现了综合性的无位姿3D场景理解。实验表明在新视角合成和语义分割上均取得优异性能。

deep_dive.title: Consistent Scene Understanding in 3D Gaussian Splatting via Multi-Cue Mask Refinement
deep_dive.abstract: Reliable instance-level scene understanding is a fundamental prerequisite for object-level interactions and high-fidelity 3D representations. While current methods often leverage 2D foundation segmentation models to obtain these priors, their 2D-centric design typically yields fragmented masks and inconsistent predictions across different views. To address these issues, we propose a novel framework that produces consistent 2D instance masks to guide the optimization of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) feature fields. Our framework consists of three main stages. (1) Multi-Cue Extraction that generates synergistic semantic, geometric, and structural priors from input images. (2) Multi-Cue-Guided Mask Merging process that consolidates fragmented masks using a composite merge score derived from semantic, depth, and edge cues. (3) Cross-View Mask Matching that establishes globally consistent identity assignments across all viewpoints. By transforming viewpoint-specific segments into coherent 3D primitives, our approach enables stable 3D instance segmentation and effective downstream editing tasks. Experiments demonstrate that our method significantly improves cross-view consistency and segmentation stability over existing baselines while maintaining high-fidelity photometric reconstruction.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: scene understanding via consistent mask refinement in 3D Gaussian splatting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过一致掩码精化在3D高斯泼溅中实现场景理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过一致掩码精化在3D高斯泼溅中实现场景理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过一致掩码精化在3D高斯泼溅中实现场景理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework using multi-cue mask refinement to produce consistent 2D instance masks for guiding 3D Gaussian Splatting feature fields, improving scene understanding.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前利用2D分割模型为3DGS提供先验的方法因视角不一致导致掩码碎片化。本文提出新框架，通过多线索提取（语义、几何、结构）引导掩码合并，生成一致的2D实例掩码，进而优化3DGS特征场。实验表明该方法显著提升了实例级场景理解的准确性和跨视角一致性。该工作为基于3DGS的可靠场景理解提供了有效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前利用2D分割模型为3DGS提供先验的方法因视角不一致导致掩码碎片化。本文提出新框架，通过多线索提取（语义、几何、结构）引导掩码合并，生成一致的2D实例掩码，进而优化3DGS特征场。实验表明该方法显著提升了实例级场景理解的准确性和跨视角一致性。该工作为基于3DGS的可靠场景理解提供了有效方案。

deep_dive.title: InvSplat: Inverse Feed-Forward Scene Splatting
deep_dive.abstract: Inverse rendering aims to recover both 3D geometry and physically meaningful material properties from images, enabling applications such as relighting and novel view synthesis. Optimization-based methods achieve high fidelity but require costly per-scene fitting, while image-space learning-based approaches often suffer from multi-view inconsistencies and lack an explicit 3D representation for stable novel view rendering. We present a feed-forward multi-view reconstruction framework for inverse rendering that directly predicts a structured 3D Gaussian representation with intrinsic material attributes. Each Gaussian primitive is parameterized by mean, normal, opacity, rotation, scale, albedo, metallic, and roughness, enabling a disentangled and physically grounded scene representation. Our model integrates priors from a material estimation network with a multi-view 3D reconstruction backbone, allowing joint prediction of geometry and reflectance parameters in a single forward pass. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate improved multi-view consistency compared to 2D baselines, accurate material recovery, and stable novel view rendering. Our representation further supports physically-based relighting and more faithful modeling of view-dependent effects compared to existing RGB-based feed-forward reconstruction methods. Our project webpage is: $\href{https://poliik.github.io/invsplat/}{\text{https://poliik.github.io/invsplat/}}$.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward inverse rendering for novel view synthesis with 3D Gaussian splatting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 前馈逆渲染实现新视角合成，结合3D高斯散点
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 前馈逆渲染实现新视角合成，结合3D高斯散点
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 前馈逆渲染实现新视角合成，结合3D高斯散点
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: InvSplat presents a feed-forward multi-view reconstruction framework for inverse rendering that directly predicts a structured 3D Gaussian representation with intrinsic material attributes, enabling novel view synthesis and relighting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出前馈式逆渲染框架InvSplat，直接从多视角图像预测具有材质属性的结构化3D高斯表示。每个高斯包含位置、法向、反照率、金属度等物理参数，支持逼真的重光照和新视角合成。相比优化方法无需逐场景拟合，相比图像空间学习保持多视角一致性。在合成和真实场景上均取得高质量渲染，为实时新视角合成和重光照提供了高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出前馈式逆渲染框架InvSplat，直接从多视角图像预测具有材质属性的结构化3D高斯表示。每个高斯包含位置、法向、反照率、金属度等物理参数，支持逼真的重光照和新视角合成。相比优化方法无需逐场景拟合，相比图像空间学习保持多视角一致性。在合成和真实场景上均取得高质量渲染，为实时新视角合成和重光照提供了高效方案。

deep_dive.title: Text-Driven 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis in Non-Manhattan Environments
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in 3D indoor synthesis for Manhattan environments. However, existing methods often fail to capture plausible object layout patterns in non-Manhattan settings, primarily because they struggle to model non-orthogonal spatial relationships, leading to high geometric violations and low physical fidelity. To address this challenge, we propose SPG-Layout, a novel text-driven framework designed to generate physically plausible indoor scenes within complex non-Manhattan environments. Specifically, we first utilize statistical priors of object distributions to guide the training process, enhancing environmental understanding and fidelity. Furthermore, mirroring human design workflows, we adopt a hierarchical layout strategy that prioritizes the placement of large objects, thereby substantially minimizing layout violations. By synergizing these components, SPG-Layout achieves a balanced optimization of semantic realism and physical plausibility. To evaluate performance in these complex settings, we constructed a new benchmark comprising 500 diverse non-Manhattan environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SPG-Layout consistently and significantly outperforms existing methods across both Manhattan and non-Manhattan environments. The code will be publicly released.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: text-driven 3D indoor scene synthesis in non-Manhattan environments
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文本驱动的非曼哈顿室内场景合成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文本驱动的非曼哈顿室内场景合成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文本驱动的非曼哈顿室内场景合成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SPG-Layout generates physically plausible 3D indoor scenes from text descriptions in complex non-Manhattan environments using statistical priors and LLM guidance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: SPG-Layout提出了一种文本驱动的3D室内场景合成框架，专门针对非曼哈顿环境中的复杂空间关系。该方法利用物体分布的统计先验引导训练过程，增强对非正交布局的理解，并模仿人类设计流程结合LLM进行布局优化。实验表明，该方法在非曼哈顿环境中生成的场景具有更低几何冲突和更高物理逼真度，有效弥补了现有方法在非曼哈顿场景合成上的空白。

deep_dive.title: PointDiT: Pixel-Space Diffusion for Monocular Geometry Estimation
deep_dive.abstract: State-of-the-art single-image 3D reconstruction methods often rely on complex hybrid architectures and loss functions, or compress geometry into latent spaces in order to leverage pre-trained latent diffusion models. In this work, we show that such architectural overhead and intricate loss formulations are unnecessary. We introduce a minimalist pixel-space Diffusion Transformer, built on a plain ViT, that operates directly on raw 3D point map patches and is conditioned on image tokens from a pre-trained DINOv3. Unlike existing latent diffusion approaches, we train our diffusion backbone entirely from scratch, eliminating the need for point map tokenizers. Despite its simplicity, our approach surpasses complex latent-based diffusion models while remaining significantly simpler than hybrid alternatives. Notably, it produces sharper geometric structure and is more robust in highly ambiguous regions, such as transparent objects.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 单目几何估计用于3D重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 单目几何估计用于3D重建
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 单目几何估计用于3D重建
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PointDiT is a pixel-space Diffusion Transformer for single-image 3D reconstruction, avoiding complex latent diffusion and achieving state-of-the-art results.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: PointDiT提出了一种像素空间扩散Transformer，直接对原始3D点图块进行操作，无需潜在扩散的复杂架构。该方法基于纯ViT架构，以DINOv3图像令牌为条件，从头训练扩散骨干，在单图像3D重建任务上超越了复杂的潜在扩散模型，同时保持了简洁性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: PointDiT提出了一种像素空间扩散Transformer，直接对原始3D点图块进行操作，无需潜在扩散的复杂架构。该方法基于纯ViT架构，以DINOv3图像令牌为条件，从头训练扩散骨干，在单图像3D重建任务上超越了复杂的潜在扩散模型，同时保持了简洁性。

deep_dive.title: WorldDirector: Building Controllable World Simulators with Persistent Dynamic Memory
deep_dive.abstract: We present WorldDirector, a highly controllable video world model framework designed for persistent dynamic object memory and unrestricted viewpoint exploration. Unlike existing world models that entangle physical dynamics with pixel rendering and rely on continuous visual observation to sustain motion, our framework explicitly decouples semantic motion orchestration from visual generation. By leveraging an LLM to coordinate 3D trajectories with camera movements and subsequently employing these orchestrated trajectories as control signals for video generation, our approach ensures strict physical logic and appearance stability, successfully preserving the exact visual identities of dynamic entities even when they re-enter the scene after prolonged periods out of view. Experimental results demonstrate that our method supports the synthesis of complex and extended events with unprecedented controllability and persistent dynamic object memory. Project Page: https://worlddirector.github.io/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: controllable video world model with dynamic object memory and viewpoint exploration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具有动态物体记忆和视点探索的可控视频世界模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具有动态物体记忆和视点探索的可控视频世界模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具有动态物体记忆和视点探索的可控视频世界模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: WorldDirector decouples semantic motion orchestration from visual generation using an LLM, enabling persistent dynamic object memory and unrestricted viewpoint exploration for video world models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: WorldDirector提出了一种高度可控的视频世界模型框架，通过显式解耦语义运动编排和视觉生成实现持久动态物体记忆和自由视点探索。利用LLM协调3D轨迹与相机运动，再将编排后的轨迹作为视频生成的控制信号，确保严格物理逻辑和外观稳定性。即使动态物体离开场景后再进入，也能保持精确视觉身份。该方法为世界模型在动态场景理解和生成中提供了新的范式。

quick_skim.title: PWM-ArtGen: Part World Model for Articulated Object Generation
quick_skim.abstract: The key challenge in articulated 3D object generation from a single image is accurately predicting the underlying kinematic structure. Existing methods either infer kinematic parameters directly from a static image that lacks dynamic part-level kinematic relationships, or estimate parameters from visual dynamics generated from a single image, which is prone to accumulated errors of two steps. Moreover, the limited scale and diversity of existing annotated datasets further hinder generalization to complex, real-world objects. To overcome these limitations, we propose to learn the joint distribution of visual dynamics and kinematic parameters. Recognizing that articulated objects can be formulated as dynamic systems, we propose a unified Part World Model called PWM-ArtGen. To leverage unannotated data, this model couples action diffusion and image diffusion with independent diffusion timesteps, which enables visual branch co-training. We further curate a photorealistic dataset of 19.7k part-level image pairs without kinematic annotations, to support co-training. Experiments demonstrate that PWM-ArtGen substantially outperforms existing baselines in the resting state and exhibits strong zero-shot generalization to out-of-distribution objects.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: part world model for articulated 3D object generation from a single image
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于部分世界模型的铰接3D物体从单图生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于部分世界模型的铰接3D物体从单图生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于部分世界模型的铰接3D物体从单图生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PWM-ArtGen proposes a Part World Model that learns the joint distribution of visual dynamics and kinematic parameters for articulated 3D object generation from a single image, achieving state-of-the-art kinematic structure prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对从单张图像生成铰接3D物体时运动学结构预测不准确的问题，提出了部分世界模型PWM-ArtGen。该模型联合学习视觉动态与运动学参数的分布，避免了分步估计的误差累积。在多个数据集上，生成的铰接物体运动学结构准确率大幅提升，且泛化到未见复杂物体，为3D内容生成提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: MindEdit-Bench: Benchmarking Object-Level Counterfactual Spatial Reasoning in VLMs from In-the-Wild Photos
quick_skim.abstract: Benchmarks for vision-language models (VLMs) mostly test observational spatial reasoning: models describe relations already visible in the input. Existing what-if tasks typically vary the observer while keeping the scene fixed. Can VLMs instead predict the consequences of hypothetically moving or rotating an object? We introduce MindEdit-Bench, a benchmark of six spatial reasoning tasks built from three-photo smartphone triplets of newly captured indoor scenes via an automatic in-the-wild 3D scene-graph extraction pipeline. Four tasks probe perception and perspective transformation over observed structure; two new tasks, L4 (spatial editing) and L5 (cross-view visibility editing), probe object-level counterfactual reasoning, where correct answers are absent from all input images. Each question provides 8-24 structured answer choices, enabling answer-letter-level diagnosis of spatial and fallback errors. The benchmark covers 120 private indoor scenes not drawn from public datasets, reducing public-data pretraining-overlap risk. Across 15 VLMs on 1,003 human-verified questions, task-wise mean VLM accuracy is only 8%-31%, versus 81%-97% human majority-vote accuracy. The pooled human--best-VLM gap is 53 pp, with at least 39 pp on every task. The structured answer space further reveals non-uniform failures, including weaker camera-depth-axis inference and fallback behavior on difficult visibility-editing cases.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D scene-graph extraction for counterfactual spatial reasoning benchmarks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用3D场景图构建反事实空间推理基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用3D场景图构建反事实空间推理基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用3D场景图构建反事实空间推理基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MindEdit-Bench introduces six spatial reasoning tasks using 3D scene-graph extraction from in-the-wild photos, probing object-level counterfactual reasoning in VLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: MindEdit-Bench提出了一个用于评估视觉语言模型（VLM）反事实空间推理能力的基准。该基准通过自动从真实世界照片中提取3D场景图，构建了六种空间推理任务，包括物体移动、旋转等反事实操作。任务L4和L5专门测试物体级别的反事实编辑和跨视角可见性推理。该工作为世界模型的3D场景理解与推理能力提供了评测标准。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: MindEdit-Bench提出了一个用于评估视觉语言模型（VLM）反事实空间推理能力的基准。该基准通过自动从真实世界照片中提取3D场景图，构建了六种空间推理任务，包括物体移动、旋转等反事实操作。任务L4和L5专门测试物体级别的反事实编辑和跨视角可见性推理。该工作为世界模型的3D场景理解与推理能力提供了评测标准。

quick_skim.title: Learning Spectral and Polarimetric Clues for One-to-Multimodal Novel View Synthesis
quick_skim.abstract: Neural rendering techniques allow for accurate reconstruction of the geometry and color appearance of 3D scenes. Some methods have extended their use to additional imaging modalities, such as multispectral, infrared, or polarimetric data. However, all of these approaches require expensive sensors and calibrated setups to capture new multimodal frames for each new scene. We propose Spectral and Polarimetric Implicit Learned Representation (SPoILeR), a novel method to obtain multi-view consistent renderings of unconventional modalities for scenes where either only RGB frames or very few of the additional modalities are available. Thanks to a multimodal pre-training phase, the model learns the mutual correlation between different modalities. This step allows predicting accurate renderings of unconventional modalities during a fine-tuning phase supervised only by RGB images. Experimental results show that the approach can accurately render infrared, polarimetric, and multispectral frames for scenes where no input sample captured by these types of sensors is provided.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: novel view synthesis from RGB to multimodal
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 新颖视图合成，从RGB到多模态

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 新颖视图合成，从RGB到多模态
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SPoILeR for one-to-multimodal novel view synthesis, learning correlations between modalities from limited multimodal data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有神经渲染方法需要昂贵的多模态传感器和标定。本文提出SPoILeR，通过多模态预训练学习模态间关联，仅需少量多模态帧即可生成多视角一致的多模态渲染结果，降低了多模态场景重建的成本。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有神经渲染方法需要昂贵的多模态传感器和标定。本文提出SPoILeR，通过多模态预训练学习模态间关联，仅需少量多模态帧即可生成多视角一致的多模态渲染结果，降低了多模态场景重建的成本。

quick_skim.title: Online Segment 3D Gaussians via Launching Virtual Drones
quick_skim.abstract: Interactive segmentation of 3D Gaussians offers a compelling opportunity for real-time manipulation of 3D scenes, thanks to the real-time rendering capability of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). However, existing methods require a time-consuming per-scene setup - typically tens of seconds or even minutes - before interactive segmentation can begin on a raw 3DGS scene. This setup involves multi-view mask preparation, mask lifting, and feature distillation, creating a major bottleneck for online applications.   To address this limitation, we aim to completely eliminate the setup stage for interactive 3DGS segmentation while keeping the segmentation time practical (under 1 second). In this work, we present SAGO (Segment Any Gaussians Online), a novel setup-free framework for interactive 3DGS segmentation. By introducing virtual drones, our method reframes the 3D segmentation problem as an online Next-Best-View (NBV) planning task formulated within a Markov process. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SAGO can extract clean 3D assets directly from 3D Gaussians with sub-second latency, thereby enabling a broad range of downstream applications such as object manipulation and scene editing. Moreover, our method achieves over a 50x speedup compared to the previous setup-free 3DGS segmentation frameworks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于3D高斯泼溅的交互式分割
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于3D高斯泼溅的交互式分割
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于3D高斯泼溅的交互式分割
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SAGO eliminates the per-scene setup for interactive 3DGS segmentation, enabling online segmentation with practical times under 1 second.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有交互式3D高斯分割方法需要耗时的逐场景设置，SAGO提出完全无设置的框架，无需多视图掩码准备和特征蒸馏。通过直接在原始3DGS场景上进行分割，将分割时间缩短至1秒以内，实现了真正的在线交互分割。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有交互式3D高斯分割方法需要耗时的逐场景设置，SAGO提出完全无设置的框架，无需多视图掩码准备和特征蒸馏。通过直接在原始3DGS场景上进行分割，将分割时间缩短至1秒以内，实现了真正的在线交互分割。
